name: Fall / Autumn
description: The autumn season — dates, traditions, harvest festivals, and seasonal facts

questions.q: In the Northern Hemisphere, autumn covers which three calendar months?

source.notes: Meteorological autumn; astronomical autumn starts at the September equinox.

questions.q: Which North American English word is the common alternative name for autumn?

source.notes: Shortened from the 16th-century phrase 'fall of the leaf'.

questions.q: On which date is Halloween observed?

source.notes: Eve of All Saints' Day.

questions.q: On which day in November does the United States celebrate Thanksgiving?

source.notes: Fixed by FDR's 1939 proclamation; signed into law 1941.

questions.q: What is the primary reason leaves on deciduous trees change color in autumn?

source.notes: Shorter days and cooler nights trigger dormancy, revealing carotenoids and anthocyanins.

questions.q: Autumn begins in March in which hemisphere?

source.notes: Runs March-May in Australia, New Zealand, and southern South America.

questions.q: Which beverage, pressed from apples and often served warm with spices, is an autumn classic in North America?

source.notes: 'Hard cider' is the fermented alcoholic version; sweet cider in the US is unfermented.

questions.q: In the Northern Hemisphere, the autumnal equinox typically falls around which date?

source.notes: Day and night are approximately equal length.

questions.q: Which baseball championship series is known as the 'Fall Classic'?

source.notes: Typically played late October-early November.

questions.q: Which ancient Celtic harvest festival is considered the origin of modern Halloween?

source.notes: Marked the end of the harvest and start of winter's 'darker half'.

questions.q: Which week-long Jewish autumn harvest festival involves building temporary shelters?

source.notes: The 'sukkah' commemorates the temporary dwellings of the Israelites in the wilderness.

questions.q: The Chinese Mid-Autumn Festival is traditionally celebrated by eating which pastry?

source.notes: Celebrated on the 15th day of the 8th lunar month.

questions.q: Which English Romantic poet wrote the 1819 ode 'To Autumn'?

source.notes: The last of Keats's six 'great odes' of 1819.

questions.q: On which Monday in October does Canada observe Thanksgiving?

source.notes: Parliament fixed the date in 1957.

questions.q: When is US Labor Day celebrated, unofficially marking the end of summer?

source.notes: Federal holiday since 1894.

questions.q: What is the popular autumn pastime of traveling to see vibrant fall foliage called?

source.notes: New England and eastern Canada are top destinations.

questions.q: Which French poet wrote 'Chanson d'automne,' whose verses were used as a signal by Allied forces during WWII?

source.notes: BBC broadcast lines of the poem to alert the French Resistance before D-Day.

questions.q: The yellow, orange, and red pigments revealed in autumn leaves as chlorophyll fades are chiefly what?

source.notes: Carotenoids yield yellow/orange; anthocyanins produce red/purple.

questions.q: On what date does the winter solstice — and the astronomical end of autumn — typically fall in the Northern Hemisphere?

source.notes: Shortest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere.

questions.q: In Old English usage, the season we now call autumn was more commonly known as what?

source.notes: The word 'autumn' entered English from Old French in the 14th century.

questions.q: In Chinese Five Elements philosophy, autumn is associated with which element?

source.notes: Also associated with the color white and the direction west.

questions.q: The American pop standard 'Autumn Leaves' was adapted from which original French song?

source.notes: Composed by Joseph Kosma with French lyrics by Jacques Prévert, 1945.
